description:
Root model for plugins.yaml file.

This file registers plugin implementations for AIPerf. Each top-level section
corresponds to a category from categories.yaml and maps type names to their
implementing classes.

Note: Package metadata (name, version, author) comes from pyproject.toml
via importlib.metadata, not from this file.

Example:
```yaml
schema_version: "1.0"

endpoint:
  my_custom:
    class: my_package.endpoints.my_custom:MyCustomEndpoint
    description: Custom endpoint for my API.
    metadata:
      endpoint_path: /v1/generate
      supports_streaming: true
      produces_tokens: true
      tokenizes_input: true
      metrics_title: My Custom Metrics

custom_dataset_loader:
  my_jsonl:
    class: my_package.dataset_loaders.my_jsonl:MyJSONLDatasetLoader
    description: Custom dataset loader for my API.
```

schema_version.description: Version of the plugins.yaml schema format. Use '1.0' for current format.
schema_version.title: Schema Version

api_router.title: API Router Plugins
api_router.description:
API routers are lifecycle-managed components that extend BaseRouter.
Each router participates in the full service lifecycle, with access
to ZMQ communication, configuration, and async initialization.
Routers expose HTTP/WebSocket endpoints via get_router() -> APIRouter.
One-to-many mapping: all registered routers are loaded.

timing_strategy.title: Timing Strategy Plugins
timing_strategy.description:
Timing strategies control request scheduling and credit issuance.
Determines when requests are sent based on fixed schedules, request rates,
or user-centric patterns. One-to-one mapping per benchmark run.

arrival_pattern.title: Arrival Pattern Plugins
arrival_pattern.description:
Interval generators determine inter-arrival times for request rate strategy.
Controls the distribution of request timing (constant, Poisson, gamma, etc.).
One-to-one mapping when using request_rate timing mode.

ramp.title: Ramp Plugins
ramp.description:
Ramp strategies control how values are gradually transitioned over time.
Used for ramping concurrency limits, request rates, and other numeric parameters.
Supports linear, exponential, and stochastic ramping patterns.

dataset_backing_store.title: Dataset Backing Store Plugins
dataset_backing_store.description:
Dataset backing stores manage conversation data on the DatasetManager side.
Provides efficient storage for dataset distribution to workers.
Handles streaming writes and finalization for client access.

dataset_client_store.title: Dataset Client Store Plugins
dataset_client_store.description:
Dataset client stores read conversation data on the Worker side.
Provides efficient access to datasets from backing stores.
Supports zero-copy reads and O(1) lookup performance.

dataset_sampler.title: Dataset Sampler Plugins
dataset_sampler.description:
Dataset samplers control how conversations are selected from the dataset.
Supports random, sequential, and shuffle sampling strategies.
One-to-one mapping based on sampling strategy configuration.

dataset_composer.title: Dataset Composer Plugins
dataset_composer.description:
Dataset composers create conversation datasets from various sources.
Handles synthetic generation, custom file loading, and specialized formats.
One-to-one mapping based on composer_type configuration.

custom_dataset_loader.title: Custom Dataset Loader Plugins
custom_dataset_loader.description:
Custom dataset loaders parse different JSONL file formats into conversations.
Supports single-turn, multi-turn, random-pool, and trace formats.
Auto-detection: loaders are tried in priority order based on can_load().

public_dataset_loader.title: Public Dataset Loader Plugins
public_dataset_loader.description:
Public dataset loaders fetch shared benchmark datasets without requiring a local file.
Supports HTTP-downloaded and HuggingFace-hosted datasets.
One-to-one mapping: selected based on --public-dataset configuration.

endpoint.title: Endpoint Plugins
endpoint.description:
Endpoints define how to format requests and parse responses for different APIs.
Supports OpenAI-compatible, HuggingFace, Cohere, NIM, and custom API formats.
One-to-one mapping based on endpoint_type configuration.

transport.title: Transport Plugins
transport.description:
Transports handle the network layer for sending requests to inference servers.
Manages connection pooling, streaming, error handling, and TCP configuration.
One-to-one mapping based on transport_type configuration.

record_processor.title: Record Processor Plugins
record_processor.description:
Record processors stream records and compute metrics in a distributed manner.
First stage of metrics pipeline, handling per-record computations.
One-to-many mapping: multiple processors can be loaded simultaneously.

results_processor.title: Results Processor Plugins
results_processor.description:
Results processors aggregate results from record processors and compute derived metrics.
Final stage of metrics pipeline for aggregated statistics and summaries.
One-to-many mapping: multiple processors can be loaded simultaneously.

gpu_telemetry_processor.title: GPU Telemetry Processor Plugins
gpu_telemetry_processor.description:
GPU telemetry processors aggregate and export GPU telemetry records locally
within GPUTelemetryManager. Side-channel pipeline that keeps telemetry
records in their origin process rather than routing through RecordsManager.
One-to-many mapping: multiple processors can be loaded simultaneously.

server_metrics_processor.title: Server Metrics Processor Plugins
server_metrics_processor.description:
Server metrics processors aggregate and export Prometheus server metrics
records locally within ServerMetricsManager. Side-channel pipeline that
keeps server metrics records in their origin process rather than routing
through RecordsManager.
One-to-many mapping: multiple processors can be loaded simultaneously.

accuracy_grader.title: Accuracy Grader Plugins
accuracy_grader.description:
Accuracy graders evaluate LLM responses against ground truth answers.
Supports exact match, math expression, multiple choice, and code execution grading.
One-to-one mapping based on grader configuration or benchmark default.

accuracy_benchmark.title: Accuracy Benchmark Plugins
accuracy_benchmark.description:
Accuracy benchmarks provide standardized problem sets for evaluating LLM correctness.
Supports MMLU, AIME, HellaSwag, BigBench, Math500, GPQA Diamond, and more.
One-to-one mapping based on accuracy benchmark configuration.

data_exporter.title: Data Exporter Plugins
data_exporter.description:
Data exporters write benchmark results to files in various formats.
Supports CSV, JSON, Parquet, and specialized export formats.
One-to-many mapping: multiple exporters can be loaded simultaneously.

console_exporter.title: Console Exporter Plugins
console_exporter.description:
Console exporters display benchmark results and diagnostics to stdout.
Provides formatted output for metrics, errors, telemetry, and traces.
One-to-many mapping: multiple console exporters can be loaded simultaneously.

ui.title: UI Plugins
ui.description:
UI components provide progress tracking and visualization during benchmark execution.
Supports rich terminal dashboards, simple progress bars, or headless execution.
One-to-one mapping based on ui configuration.

url_selection_strategy.title: Url Selection Strategy Plugins
url_selection_strategy.description:
URL selection strategies control how requests are distributed across multiple endpoints.
Supports round-robin, random, and custom distribution patterns.
One-to-one mapping based on url_selection_strategy configuration.

service.title: Service Plugins
service.description:
Services are the core processes that make up the AIPerf distributed system.
Each service runs in a separate process and communicates via ZMQ message bus.
Includes SystemController, Workers, Managers, and processing services.

service_manager.title: Service Manager Plugins
service_manager.description:
Service managers orchestrate how services are launched and managed.
Supports multiprocessing (single-node) and Kubernetes (multi-node) deployments.
One-to-one mapping based on run_mode configuration.

communication.title: Communication Plugins
communication.description:
Communication backends provide the underlying transport for inter-service messaging.
Supports ZMQ IPC (single-node) and ZMQ TCP (multi-node) communication.
One-to-one mapping based on deployment configuration.

communication_client.title: Communication Client Plugins
communication_client.description:
Communication clients implement different ZMQ socket patterns for messaging.
Includes PUB/SUB, PUSH/PULL, REQUEST/REPLY, and streaming patterns.
Internal infrastructure: automatically created by framework based on usage.

zmq_proxy.title: ZMQ Proxy Plugins
zmq_proxy.description:
ZMQ proxies provide message routing between different socket patterns.
Includes XPUB/XSUB, DEALER/ROUTER, and PUSH/PULL proxy types.
Internal infrastructure: automatically created by framework based on configuration.

plot.title: Plot Plugins
plot.description:
Plot handlers create different types of visualizations from benchmark data.
Supports scatter, histogram, timeline, percentile bands, and multi-run comparisons.
One-to-one mapping based on plot type selection.

gpu_telemetry_collector.title: GPU Telemetry Collector Plugins
gpu_telemetry_collector.description:
GPU telemetry collectors gather GPU metrics from various sources and deliver them via callbacks.
Supports DCGM HTTP endpoints and plugin-defined local collectors.
One-to-one mapping based on gpu_telemetry_collector configuration.

search_recipe.title: Search Recipe Plugins
search_recipe.description:
Search recipes are named, plugin-registered presets that compile down to
AdaptiveSearchSweep (Bayesian Optimization) or sweep.parameters (grid) in
the CLI converter (`aiperf.config.flags._converter_optionals.expand_search_recipe`,
lifted onto the top-level sweep envelope by `aiperf.config.flags.converter
._apply_recipe_sweep_parameters`). Selected via `--search-recipe <name>` on
the CLI. One-to-one mapping per benchmark run.

search_recipe_post_process.title: Search Recipe Post Process Plugins
search_recipe_post_process.description:
Post-process handlers run after `SweepAnalyzer.compute()` in
`aggregate_sweep_and_export` and emit derived artifacts (curves, knee
points) into `sweep_aggregate/`. Handlers are stateless and one instance
is reused per recipe. Selected indirectly via a recipe's PostProcessSpec.
One-to-one mapping per recipe-emitted PostProcessSpec.

convergence_criterion.title: Convergence Criterion Plugins
convergence_criterion.description:
Convergence criteria decide when benchmark metrics have stabilized across
repeated runs and the adaptive trial loop can stop. Each criterion declares
its statistical assumptions (min samples, confidence-level usage, JSONL
consumption) via metadata so the CLI can validate `--convergence-*` flags
before importing scipy/statsmodels. Selected via `--convergence-mode <name>`.
One-to-one mapping per benchmark run.

search_planner.title: Search Planner Plugins
search_planner.description:
Search planners drive the adaptive outer loop, proposing the next variation
via `ask()` and absorbing observed metrics via `tell()`. Each declares
dimension-type and objective-direction support via metadata so the CLI can
validate `--search-space` shape before importing the planner's heavy
soft-dep tree (optuna, botorch, scipy, etc.). Selected via `--search-planner <name>`.
One-to-one mapping per benchmark run.

title: PluginsManifest

class.description: Python class that implements this plugin entry. Use 'module.path:ClassName' format, e.g., 'aiperf.endpoints.openai_chat:ChatEndpoint'.
class.title: Class

description.description: Brief explanation of what this plugin type does and when to use it.
description.title: Description

priority.description: Conflict resolution priority. When multiple packages register the same type name, the one with higher priority wins. Use 0 for normal plugins, higher values to override built-in implementations.
priority.title: Priority

metadata.description: Category-specific configuration for this plugin type. The allowed fields depend on the category's metadata_class in categories.yaml.
metadata.title: Metadata

metadata.description:
Metadata schema for custom dataset loader plugins.

Defines format-specific defaults for dataset loaders. When a loader specifies
``block_size``, it overrides the user's ``--isl-block-size`` config default,
ensuring hash-based prompt generation uses the correct token block size for the
trace format (e.g. 16 for Bailian, 512 for Mooncake).

Referenced by: categories.yaml custom_dataset_loader.metadata_class
Used in: plugins.yaml custom_dataset_loader entries

is_trace.description: Whether this loader handles trace-format datasets. Trace datasets use hash_ids-based prompt generation, support synthesis options, and prefer sequential sampling with fixed_schedule timing.
is_trace.title: Is Trace

default_block_size.description: Default token block size for hash-based prompt caching. Used when the user does not explicitly set --isl-block-size. Must match the block size used to generate the trace's hash_ids (e.g. 16 for Bailian, 512 for Mooncake).
default_block_size.title: Default Block Size

category.description: Filter dataset rows to a specific category value. Used by custom loaders that support per-category subsets (e.g. SPEED-Bench).
category.title: Category

multi_turn.description: When true, each row becomes one Conversation with multiple Turn objects.
multi_turn.title: Multi Turn

metadata.title: CustomDatasetLoaderMetadata

CustomDatasetLoaderPlugin.title: Custom Dataset Loader Plugin
CustomDatasetLoaderPlugin.description:
Custom dataset loaders parse different JSONL file formats into conversations.
Supports single-turn, multi-turn, random-pool, and trace formats.
Auto-detection: loaders are tried in priority order based on can_load().

class.title: Class

description.title: Description

priority.description: Conflict resolution priority. When multiple packages register the same type name, the one with higher priority wins. Use 0 for normal plugins, higher values to override built-in implementations.
priority.title: Priority

metadata.description:
Metadata schema for public dataset loader plugins.

Referenced by: categories.yaml public_dataset_loader.metadata_class
Used in: plugins.yaml public_dataset_loader entries

hf_dataset_name.description: HuggingFace dataset identifier (e.g. 'AI-MO/NuminaMath-TIR'). Required for HF-backed loaders.
hf_dataset_name.title: Hf Dataset Name

hf_subset.description: HuggingFace dataset subset/config name. Only needed for datasets with multiple configs.
hf_subset.title: Hf Subset

prompt_column.description: Column name containing the prompt/instruction text. Required for HFInstructionResponseDatasetLoader.
prompt_column.title: Prompt Column

image_column.description: Column name containing the image data (PIL Image). Used for multimodal datasets.
image_column.title: Image Column

video_column.description: Column name containing the video data (URL string or bytes dict). Used for video multimodal datasets.
video_column.title: Video Column

audio_column.description: Column name containing audio data. Depending on loader/dataset, this can be an HF Audio dict with array/sampling_rate (decoded) or bytes/path (decode=False). Used for ASR/speech datasets.
audio_column.title: Audio Column

conversation_column.description: Column name containing the conversation messages array. Required for HFConversationDatasetLoader.
conversation_column.title: Conversation Column

message_content_key.description: Key inside each message dict for the text content. Used with conversation_column (e.g. 'content', 'value').
message_content_key.title: Message Content Key

multi_turn.description: When true, each row becomes one Conversation with multiple Turn objects. For HFConversationDatasetLoader: user→assistant (OpenAI roles) or human→gpt (ShareGPT-style ``from`` fields) pairs.
multi_turn.title: Multi Turn

streaming.description: Whether to load the HuggingFace dataset in streaming mode. Use true for large datasets (>10 GB) to avoid downloading the full dataset. Use false (default) for small datasets to leverage HF caching and len() support.
streaming.title: Streaming

has_timing_data.description: Whether the loader emits timestamps suitable for fixed-schedule replay.
has_timing_data.title: Has Timing Data

prompt_template.description: Python str.format() template for constructing the prompt from multiple columns (e.g. '{code}\n\n{change_request}'). When set, overrides prompt_column. All referenced column names must exist in the dataset.
prompt_template.title: Prompt Template

metadata.title: PublicDatasetLoaderMetadata

metadata.description:
Metadata schema for endpoint plugins.

Defines API capabilities, paths, and multimodal support for endpoint implementations.
Used by the framework to route requests, configure streaming, and enable
multimodal inputs/outputs (images, audio, video).

Referenced by: categories.yaml endpoint.metadata_class
Used in: plugins.yaml endpoint entries

streaming_path.description: Streaming API path if different from the endpoint path (e.g., /generate_stream).
streaming_path.title: Streaming Path

service_kind.description: The service kind of the endpoint (used for artifact naming).
service_kind.title: Service Kind

requires_polling.description: Whether endpoint uses async job polling (submit job, poll for status, retrieve result).
requires_polling.title: Requires Polling

requires_form_data.description: True for endpoints that require multipart/form-data (binary uploads), e.g., video_generation, image_edit. EndpointConfig consumes this flag to auto-select the request encoding.
requires_form_data.title: Requires Form Data

requires_inline_media.description: Whether endpoint requires media URLs to be downloaded and inlined as base64 data URLs.
requires_inline_media.title: Requires Inline Media

metadata.title: EndpointMetadata

url_schemes.description: URL schemes this transport handles (for auto-detection and validation).
url_schemes.title: Url Schemes

AccuracyGraderPlugin.title: Accuracy Grader Plugin
AccuracyGraderPlugin.description:
Accuracy graders evaluate LLM responses against ground truth answers.
Supports exact match, math expression, multiple choice, and code execution grading.
One-to-one mapping based on grader configuration or benchmark default.

metadata.description:
Metadata schema for service plugins.

Defines lifecycle and runtime configuration for AIPerf distributed services.
Used by SystemController to manage service startup order and optimize
latency-sensitive services (timing, workers) by disabling garbage collection.

Referenced by: categories.yaml service.metadata_class
Used in: plugins.yaml service entries

required.description: Whether the service is required for benchmark execution.
required.title: Required

auto_start.description: Whether the service is automatically started by the system controller.
auto_start.title: Auto Start

disable_gc.description: Whether to disable garbage collection in the service for timing-critical operations.
disable_gc.title: Disable Gc

replicable.description: Whether the service can have multiple instances running in parallel.
replicable.title: Replicable

metadata.title: ServiceMetadata

ServicePlugin.title: Service Plugin
ServicePlugin.description:
Services are the core processes that make up the AIPerf distributed system.
Each service runs in a separate process and communicates via ZMQ message bus.
Includes SystemController, Workers, Managers, and processing services.

ServiceManagerPlugin.description:

Supports multiprocessing (single-node) and Kubernetes (multi-node) deployments.

CommunicationPlugin.description:

Supports ZMQ IPC (single-node) and ZMQ TCP (multi-node) communication.

CommunicationClientPlugin.title: Communication Client Plugin
CommunicationClientPlugin.description:
Communication clients implement different ZMQ socket patterns for messaging.
Includes PUB/SUB, PUSH/PULL, REQUEST/REPLY, and streaming patterns.
Internal infrastructure: automatically created by framework based on usage.

ZmqProxyPlugin.description:
ZMQ proxies provide message routing between different socket patterns.
Includes XPUB/XSUB, DEALER/ROUTER, and PUSH/PULL proxy types.

Supports scatter, histogram, timeline, percentile bands, and multi-run comparisons.
One-to-one mapping based on plot type selection.

metadata.description:
Config-time metadata for GPU telemetry collector plugins.

Local collectors set ``is_local`` so ``--gpu-telemetry <name>`` is treated
as an in-process collector keyword instead of a DCGM URL. Native binding
validation lives on the collector class itself via ``validate_environment``
so each implementation owns its own dependency check.

Referenced by: categories.yaml gpu_telemetry_collector.metadata_class
Used in: plugins.yaml gpu_telemetry_collector entries

is_local.description: Whether this collector runs in-process against the local host.
is_local.title: Is Local

metadata.title: GPUTelemetryCollectorMetadata

GpuTelemetryCollectorPlugin.title: GPU Telemetry Collector Plugin
GpuTelemetryCollectorPlugin.description:
GPU telemetry collectors gather GPU metrics from various sources and deliver them via callbacks.
Supports DCGM HTTP endpoints and plugin-defined local collectors.
One-to-one mapping based on gpu_telemetry_collector configuration.

SearchRecipePlugin.title: Search Recipe Plugin
SearchRecipePlugin.description:
Search recipes are named, plugin-registered presets that compile down to
AdaptiveSearchSweep (Bayesian Optimization) or sweep.parameters (grid) in
the CLI converter (`aiperf.config.flags._converter_optionals.expand_search_recipe`,
lifted onto the top-level sweep envelope by `aiperf.config.flags.converter
._apply_recipe_sweep_parameters`). Selected via `--search-recipe <name>` on
the CLI. One-to-one mapping per benchmark run.

class.description: Python class that implements this plugin entry. Use 'module.path:ClassName' format, e.g., 'aiperf.endpoints.openai_chat:ChatEndpoint'.
class.title: Class

description.title: Description

priority.title: Priority

metadata.title: Metadata

SearchRecipePostProcessPlugin.title: Search Recipe Post Process Plugin
SearchRecipePostProcessPlugin.description:
Post-process handlers run after `SweepAnalyzer.compute()` in
`aggregate_sweep_and_export` and emit derived artifacts (curves, knee
points) into `sweep_aggregate/`. Handlers are stateless and one instance
is reused per recipe. Selected indirectly via a recipe's PostProcessSpec.
One-to-one mapping per recipe-emitted PostProcessSpec.

metadata.description:
Metadata schema for convergence criterion plugins.

Declares statistical-method capabilities so the CLI/config layer can
validate `--convergence-metric` / `--convergence-stat` against the chosen
criterion before the plugin is imported.

Referenced by: categories.yaml convergence_criterion.metadata_class
Used in: plugins.yaml convergence_criterion entries

min_samples.description: Minimum number of successful runs required before convergence can trigger.
min_samples.title: Min Samples

requires_confidence_level.description: Whether the criterion consumes plan.confidence_level (e.g. CI-width does, CV doesn't).
requires_confidence_level.title: Requires Confidence Level

requires_jsonl_export.description: Whether the criterion reads per-request metrics from JSONL exports (e.g. distribution does).
requires_jsonl_export.title: Requires Jsonl Export

metric_kinds.description: Kinds of metrics this criterion handles. One or more of 'continuous', 'counts', 'categorical'.
metric_kinds.title: Metric Kinds

metadata.title: ConvergenceCriterionMetadata

ConvergenceCriterionPlugin.title: Convergence Criterion Plugin
ConvergenceCriterionPlugin.description:
Convergence criteria decide when benchmark metrics have stabilized across
repeated runs and the adaptive trial loop can stop. Each criterion declares
its statistical assumptions (min samples, confidence-level usage, JSONL
consumption) via metadata so the CLI can validate `--convergence-*` flags
before importing scipy/statsmodels. Selected via `--convergence-mode <name>`.
One-to-one mapping per benchmark run.

class.description: Python class that implements this plugin entry. Use 'module.path:ClassName' format, e.g., 'aiperf.endpoints.openai_chat:ChatEndpoint'.
class.title: Class

description.description: Brief explanation of what this plugin type does and when to use it.
description.title: Description

priority.description: Conflict resolution priority. When multiple packages register the same type name, the one with higher priority wins. Use 0 for normal plugins, higher values to override built-in implementations.
priority.title: Priority

metadata.description:
Metadata schema for search planner plugins.

Declares dimension-type and objective-direction support so the CLI/config
layer can validate `--search-space` shape against the chosen planner
before the planner (and its heavy soft-dep imports) is loaded.

Referenced by: categories.yaml search_planner.metadata_class
Used in: plugins.yaml search_planner entries

supports_continuous.description: Whether the planner accepts Real-valued search-space dimensions.
supports_continuous.title: Supports Continuous

supports_discrete.description: Whether the planner accepts Integer-valued search-space dimensions.
supports_discrete.title: Supports Discrete

supports_categorical.description: Whether the planner accepts Categorical search-space dimensions.
supports_categorical.title: Supports Categorical

requires_initial_samples.description: Minimum number of initial random/Sobol samples required before the planner's model is fit. None when the planner has no warm-up phase.
requires_initial_samples.title: Requires Initial Samples

compatible_objective_directions.description: Objective directions the planner can optimize. Lower-case strings.
compatible_objective_directions.title: Compatible Objective Directions

requires_extras.description: Names of pyproject.toml extras (e.g. ['optuna']) required to install this planner's heavy dependencies. Informational; the planner class itself owns the ImportError surface.
requires_extras.title: Requires Extras

metadata.title: SearchPlannerMetadata

SearchPlannerPlugin.title: Search Planner Plugin
SearchPlannerPlugin.description:
Search planners drive the adaptive outer loop, proposing the next variation
via `ask()` and absorbing observed metrics via `tell()`. Each declares
dimension-type and objective-direction support via metadata so the CLI can
validate `--search-space` shape before importing the planner's heavy
soft-dep tree (optuna, botorch, scipy, etc.). Selected via `--search-planner <name>`.
One-to-one mapping per benchmark run.
